mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that has been flat or declining over the trailing three months signals accommodative monetary conditions that lower the discount rate on future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other regime conditions.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following flat/declining FEDFUNDS periods are not statistically higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window after a period of historic lows, it signals an inflection in the labor cycle that precedes broad earnings deceleration, causing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days to turn negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over trailing 3 months from a cycle low
failure_condition: SPX 20d forward returns are positive or not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) in more than 55% of triggered episodes across the historical walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) and a still-elevated or rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly indicate that monetary policy remains restrictive even as labor demand weakens, amplifying recession risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days) beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS is flat or rising
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor softening
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-deceleration/FEDFUNDS-restrictive signal shows no incremental predictive power over the single-series PAYEMS model (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of expanding real GDP, positive month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer demand that supports corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; this relationship weakens or reverses outside expansionary regimes.
regime_hint: GDP expansion regime (positive trailing GDPC1 growth)
failure_condition: RSAFS momentum shows no significant positive correlation with forward 20d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods when trailing GDPC1 growth is positive, across a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: A short-term acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) month-over-month signals improving manufacturing throughput that is quickly priced into equities within one week, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward SPX return following INDPRO acceleration triggers is not significantly different from the unconditional 5-day mean return (p > 0.05) over rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 10% from its trailing 12-month peak signals contraction in interest-rate-sensitive demand that historically leads broader economic slowdowns by two to three quarters, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from trailing 12-month peak
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening financial conditions
failure_condition: In more than half of historical HOUST drawdown episodes of this magnitude, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains below the trailing 12-month CPI year-over-year rate (implying negative real rates), markets interpret this combination as policy accommodation despite inflation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; if FEDFUNDS instead rises above CPI growth, the relationship inverts to negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL YoY inflation rate
failure_condition: The sign of forward 20d SPX returns does not flip between negative-real-rate and positive-real-rate regimes as predicted, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects ongoing labor market strength that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as an always-on momentum signal.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month growth and forward 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling walk-forward window.
